commit.message:
Fix camera: use device ID enumeration instead of facingMode constraints

The facingMode constraint (both 'environment' and 'user') fails on
many Mac laptops. New approach:
1. Request camera permission with plain getUserMedia({video:true})
2. Enumerate cameras with Html5Qrcode.getCameras()
3. Pick rear camera by label if available, otherwise first camera
4. Start scanner with the device ID directly (no facingMode)

fix(test): resolve type and lint errors in task-file-watcher.test.ts (#665)

This PR fixes the type check and lint errors reported in CI monitor
(Issue #663).

## Changes

1. **Unused variable fix**: Renamed `watchClosed` to `_watchClosed` and
   added `void _watchClosed` statement to mark it as intentionally used
   (it's tracked by MockFSWatcher.close())

2. **Unused parameter fix**: Renamed `options` to `_options` in readdir
   mock function

3. **Redundant async fix**: Removed async keywords from mock functions
   that don't use await, replacing with explicit Promise.resolve() and
   Promise.reject() returns

4. **Type mismatch fix**: Used vi.mocked() wrapper in expect() calls
   to properly type the mock functions for toHaveBeenCalled* assertions

5. **beforeEach/afterEach async fix**: Removed unnecessary async keywords
   from beforeEach and afterEach hooks

## Test Results

- All 11 tests pass
- TypeScript type check passes
- ESLint passes (only pre-existing warnings remain)

Fixes #663

commit.message:
Restructure hunting-grounds skill with phased execution protocol (P6) (#82)

Replaces flat "Required Tool Calls" section with a query triage and
phased execution model that handles three query types distinctly:

- System-specific: always query the named system first (Phase 1a)
  before showing nearby alternatives
- Discovery: go straight to hotspots search
- Territory: resolve coalition systems via territory_analysis before
  hotspot search

Adds a worked example for "analyze Rancer" to anchor correct behavior,
and explicit anti-patterns for the subject-system-skipping failure mode
seen in exercise run P6.

commit.message:
feat: add data quality assertions to statements extraction pipeline (#1696)

* feat: add data quality assertions to statements extraction pipeline

Adds a new `validate-quality.ts` module with three core exports:
- `validateExtractedStatements()` — checks post-LLM extraction output
- `validateCreateStatementBatch()` — checks pre-DB-write batch
- `printQualityReport()` — color-coded CLI reporter

Assertion checks:
- ZERO_STATEMENTS: no statements from a page with ≥2 sections
- EMPTY_STATEMENT_TEXT: blank statement text strings
- NON_FINITE_NUMERIC: NaN or Infinity in numeric values
- ALL_ZERO_NUMERIC: all-zero batch when ≥3 numeric values (suggests extraction failure)

In dry-run mode, violations are warnings (logged but not blocking).
In apply mode (--apply flag), violations abort before any DB writes.

Integration points:
- `extract.ts`: validates after FK sanitization, before dry-run/apply split
- `improve.ts`: validates accepted statements before `createStatementBatch()`

Closes #1653

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  items with the same (subjectEntityId, propertyId, valueDate) key

commit.message:
fix: resolve issue #38 — only fall back to all-messages for Six/Connect groups (#39)

WhatsApp groups use replace-mode uploads (all messages get a fresh inserted_at
on each scrape). When the 24h filter returns nothing, it means no recent scrape
or no new activity — falling back to all messages produces a summary of stale
historical data, which is misleading.

Gate the all-messages fallback on source being "Six" or "Connect" (append-mode
groups that keep original message timestamps). WhatsApp groups now skip cleanly
with a log line when the 24h window is empty.

Also expose the `source` field in the /api/groups response so summariser.py
can read it without an extra API call.

Co-authored-by: Emilian <emilian@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive audit remediation — regulatory compliance & security

Payroll Engine (payroll.engine.ts):
- Fix NI employee rates: 0.4% → 3.5% (below threshold), 7% → 12% (above)
- Fix NI lower threshold: 7,522 → 7,700 NIS (2026)
- Fix minimum wage: 6,300 → 5,880 NIS monthly, 33.62 → 33.91 hourly
- Fix training fund taxable excess formula: Math.min → Math.max(0, rate-4.5%)
- Add recuperation rate verification comment

Security (app.ts, users.routes.ts):
- Fix CORS: prevent wildcard + credentials vulnerability
- Add correlation ID to error responses, hide stack traces in production
- Fix login password minimum: min(1) → min(8)
- Add rate limiter (5/15min) on forgot-password endpoint

Accounting (accounting.service.ts, reports.service.ts):
- Strengthen double-entry validator: require non-null account IDs
- Tighten trial balance tolerance: 0.01 → 0.001
- Add console.error warning when balance sheet is unbalanced
- Expose balanceDifference in balance sheet response

Invoices (invoices.service.ts):
- Fix VAT calculation: per-line rates instead of first-line only
- Fix cancelInvoice: auto-void linked journal entry (חשבוניות ישראל)
- Add guard: explicit error when required GL accounts are missing
- Fix AR aging filter: notIn DRAFT/PAID/CANCELLED (explicit)

Purchasing (purchasing.service.ts):
- Fix VAT calculation: per-line rates instead of hardcoded 18%

Labor Law (attendance.routes.ts, employees.routes.ts, hr/leave.routes.ts):
- Enforce max 10 working hours/day (חוק שעות עבודה ומנוחה)
- Enforce 8-hour minimum rest between shifts
- Add minimum wage warning header on employee creation
- Add leave balance check before approving leave requests
- Add 7-consecutive-days advisory warning for annual leave

Schema (prisma/schema.prisma):
- Add soft-delete (deletedAt) to Invoice, Transaction, Payslip, PayrollRun, Bill
- Add vatNumber @db.VarChar(20) with proper type to Tenant
- Add invoiceType, creditNoteForId, version to Invoice model
- Add LeaveBalance, MaternityLeave, Termination models
- Add missing indexes on tenantId fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: v1.8.0 MCP orchestration tools, browser read-only, WS state sync

Phase 1 — Bug fixes + browser read-only:
- Fix canvas widget id duplication crash (WidgetState got id twice)
- Remove ensureOrchestrator auto-call from frontend navigation
  (browser is now purely read-only — no side effects from clicking)
- New agent_state_sync WS event for reconnect state hydration
  (distinct from agent_spawned — no duplicate sections on reconnect)
- Frontend handleStateSync() clears stale sections, rebuilds from server

Phase 2 — MCP orchestration tools:
- New orchestrator MCP server (backend/mcp/orchestrator_server.py)
  with explicit tools: list_tasks, dispatch_agent, get_agents,
  report_complete, dispatch_custom
- New mcp-orchestrator docker-compose service on port 4042
- Combined controller MCP config (canvas + orchestrator)
- Controller prompts updated to reference MCP tools instead of
  Agent tool — clear hierarchy: orchestrator dispatches via MCP,
  workers execute autonomously
- Fix canvas MCP server to use SSE transport (was stdio, kept crashing)
- New /api/projects/{name}/tasks/{task_index}/complete endpoint

Add auto-update of ADO work item state and comments during planning and completion

- Add getTerminalState() to AdoClient with in-memory caching for auto-detecting
  terminal states (Done/Closed/Completed) from ADO work item type transitions
- workitem-plan now transitions state to "In Progress" and posts comments (with
  --no-update flag to skip), matching code-plan behavior
- code-plan comment now includes full plan guidance text instead of just timestamp
- work-items update --complete auto-detects terminal state and posts completion comment
- Update command docs with new flags and side effects sections
- Add tests for getTerminalState and workitem-plan side effects

Add VM0044 Biochar Carbon Projects course (12 lessons, 12 quizzes)

Full course covering VM0044 v1.2 methodology for biochar utilization
in soil and non-soil applications. 5 modules: Foundations, Eligibility,
Project Boundary and Additionality, Quantifying Carbon Removals, and

permanence factors, worked examples, and CalculationExercise components.

commit.message:
Add MACHTwoChannel: two-channel modulation + writing architecture

Channel 1 (modulation): learned activation primitives with continuous scalar
weights for natural interpolation. Channel 2 (writing): existing basis vector
patch system with higher metabolic cost penalty. Both driven by shared
task_state from DemoProjection. ~654K params.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove 2D track mode, add human mask augmentation, move configs to y2r/

- Remove track_type parameter: models, dataloaders, training, visualization,
  and configs are now 3D-only (UVD)
- Add human mask detection in SAM3 pipeline (second "person" prompt round)
  and store in HDF5 for training-time augmentation
- GPU augmentations support human-specific color jitter and noise
- Move training configs from configs/ to y2r/configs/
- Simplify SAM3 mask format from (T,O,H,W) to merged (T,H,W) boolean
